claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions. Child Benefit eligibility is assessed for each child separately.

**First, do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how old they are?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who is 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Let me continue assessing your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, who is 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is 7 years old (under 16), they automatically qualify as a qualifying child in terms of age. Now I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

**Are you responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I currently live abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. This is an important factor for Child Benefit eligibility.

**Could you tell me which country you currently live in?** 

Also, **are you or your partner working for an organization such as:**
- The UK civil service
- UK armed forces
- A UK-based company with offices abroad

This matters because there are some exceptions to the UK residency requirement.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in Spain. And no, neither I nor my partner work for the UK civil service, UK armed forces, or a UK-based company with offices abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me check one more thing about your situation.

**Are you (or your partner if you have one) receiving any UK State Pension or other UK benefits?** 

Also, **have you or your partner been posted to Spain by a UK government department or the armed forces?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us are receiving any UK State Pension or other UK benefits. And no, we haven't been posted to Spain by a UK government department or the armed forces. We just moved here on our own.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me gather the final details to complete the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

**Assessment for Alex (age 7):**

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex.

**Reason:** 

One of the fundamental eligibility requirements for Child Benefit is that you must be living in the UK (or have a qualifying connection to the UK, such as working for the UK civil service, armed forces, or certain UK government positions abroad).

Since you:
- Live in Spain
- Are not posted there by the UK government or armed forces
- Are not working for the UK civil service, armed forces, or a UK-based company
- Are not receiving UK State Pension or other UK benefits

You do not meet the UK residency requirement for Child Benefit, even though Alex is your qualifying child in all other respects.

**Important note:** If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK, or if you establish a qualifying UK connection while living abroad, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit at that time.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that to me. I understand now that I'm not eligible because I don't live in the UK. That's helpful to know - I appreciate your time!
